steps.title: Identify where two independent clauses meet
steps.content: The sentence contains two complete thoughts (independent clauses): “Her work challenges conventional notions of race, gender, history, and [blank]” and “she is credited with expanding the horizons of conceptual photographic art.” In the same sentence, these need proper connection.

steps.title: Apply the joining rule
steps.content: A comma with a coordinating conjunction joins two clauses in the same sentence. Choice B (“memory, and”) uses this structure correctly: “...and memory, and she is credited with...” The first “and” finishes the list of notions; the second “and” (after the comma) is the coordinating conjunction that joins the two clauses. Choices A, C, and D omit the necessary comma, conjunction, or both. That matches choice B — B is correct.
